title: Cannot Access Shared Folder
cardText: Diagnose problems accessing network shared folders.
Check network connectivity, server availability, permissions, credentials, DNS, and sharing settings.
Separate connectivity issues from access and permission problems.
text: This guide is for situations where a shared network folder cannot be opened.
Determine whether the issue affects one user, one computer, or everyone.
Escalate server or permission issues when local troubleshooting does not resolve the problem.

checkAffectedUsers.title: Check who is affected
body: Determine whether only one user is affected or whether multiple users cannot access the shared folder.
This helps identify whether the issue is local to one device or affects the server or share itself.

checkInternetConnectivity.title: Check network connectivity
body: Confirm the computer can access the local network and internet.
A disconnected device cannot reach shared resources.

networkIssue.title: Resolve network connectivity
body: The computer is not connected to the network.
Restore network connectivity before attempting to access shared folders.

checkServerAvailability.title: Check server availability
body: Determine whether the computer hosting the shared folder is powered on and reachable.
If the server is offline, the share cannot be accessed.

serverOffline.title: Server unavailable
body: The shared folder host is not available.
Power on the server or restore connectivity before continuing.

testServerName.title: Access share by server name
body: Attempt to access the share using the server name.
Example: \\SERVERNAME\Share
This tests DNS and name resolution.

shareAccessible.title: Share accessible
body: The shared folder can be reached using the server name.
No further troubleshooting is required.

testServerIp.title: Access share by IP address
body: Try accessing the share using the server IP address.
Example: \\192.168.1.10\Share
This bypasses name resolution.

dnsIssue.title: DNS or name resolution issue
body: The share works by IP address but not by server name.
Investigate DNS, hostnames, network discovery, or local name resolution settings.

checkPermissions.title: Check access permissions
body: Determine whether the user has permission to access the folder.
Permission errors often appear as Access Denied messages.

permissionIssue.title: Permission issue found
body: The user does not have sufficient permissions.
Review share permissions and NTFS permissions if applicable.
Grant access only as appropriate.

checkCredentials.title: Check saved credentials
body: Incorrect saved credentials can prevent access.
Remove saved credentials and reconnect if necessary.
Verify the correct username and password are being used.

credentialIssue.title: Credential issue found
body: Incorrect or expired credentials are preventing access.
Update saved credentials and reconnect to the share.

checkSharingSettings.title: Check sharing configuration
body: Verify the folder is still shared.
Check that file and printer sharing is enabled.
Review any recent configuration changes.

fixSharing.title: Correct sharing settings
body: The folder is not being shared correctly.
Reconfigure sharing settings and test access again.

checkFirewall.title: Check firewall settings
body: Firewall rules can block file sharing traffic.
Confirm file and printer sharing is allowed through the firewall.

adjustFirewall.title: Adjust firewall configuration
body: The firewall is preventing access to the shared folder.
Allow the required sharing services and retest.

finalEscalation.title: Further server diagnostics required
body: Basic network, DNS, permissions, credentials, sharing, and firewall checks did not resolve the issue.
Investigate server logs, Active Directory, SMB configuration, storage availability, or operating system issues.
Escalate for server-side diagnostics.
